title: 5V/1A USB Charger
description: A basic synchronous buck converter converting a 12 V wall adapter to a 5 V USB output rail. Optimised for simplicity and low BOM cost at 200 kHz.
design_notes: The 200 kHz switching frequency is a deliberate choice for beginners: low enough that the inductor and capacitor are large enough to solder by hand and measure with a standard oscilloscope. The 30 % ripple ratio (ΔIL = 0.30 A) keeps the converter comfortably in CCM at full load and gives about 2× headroom above the CCM/DCM boundary. A 47 µF ceramic output capacitor easily meets the 50 mV ripple target. Note that USB Quick Charge or PD protocols require additional negotiation ICs not modelled here — this design produces a fixed, unregulated-protocol 5 V rail.
source: TI SLVA477B — Switching Regulator Fundamentals; Erickson & Maksimovic §2.3
